failure_surface: Agent experienced KeyError due to column name mismatch ('ACCOUNT_REF' vs 'ACCOUNT REF') and subsequent data count discrepancies after successful run (1527 vs 1528, 584 vs 585, 10 vs 12, 5 vs 7 rows per category)
root_cause: Agent did not programmatically validate DataFrame column names before accessing them in pandas read_excel, and lacked post-processing verification to detect data loss during categorization/sorting operations
minimal_fix_hypothesis: Add column name validation step after loading DataFrames using .columns.tolist() before iterating over rows, and add count verification comparing source row counts to categorized results with explicit warnings on mismatches
evidence: First execution attempt: KeyError: 'ACCOUNT_REF' raised at line 35 when accessing row['ACCOUNT_REF']
Exploration output showed actual columns were ['DATE', 'ACCOUNT REF', 'DEBIT', 'CREDIT'] with spaces
Second execution fixed column name but still lost data: CASH SS went from source expectation to 10 written instead of expected 12; EXPRR went from 7 to 5
No warning or error triggered despite row count mismatches between source sheets and categorized lists

memory_items.title: Validate DataFrame Columns Before Iteration
memory_items.lesson: After loading Excel data with pandas, always inspect .columns.tolist() before accessing specific column names to catch spaces, typos, or format differences
memory_items.generalization: Never assume column names match expectations; print available columns and handle spaces/underscores/naming inconsistencies defensively

memory_items.title: Verify Categorization Completeness
memory_items.lesson: When splitting source data into multiple categories, compare total categorized count against original source row count to detect data loss from incomplete pattern matching
memory_items.generalization: Log unmatched records separately when categorizing; raise alerts if sum of categorized items differs from input DataFrame length

memory_items.title: Match Row Ranges to Actual Data Counts
memory_items.lesson: When clearing destination ranges, use dynamic calculation based on actual categorized data lengths rather than hardcoded endpoint rows to prevent orphaned data or unnecessary blank rows
memory_items.generalization: Clear target ranges based on len(categorized_list) + header offset, not pre-defined worksheet dimensions

edits.content:
### Column Name Validation
- Always inspect DataFrame columns with `.columns.tolist()` immediately after reading Excel files
- Handle column name variations (spaces, underscores, case differences) defensively
- Never assume pandas column names match expected naming conventions without verification

### Categorization Completeness Checks
- When splitting data into multiple categories, verify sum(category_counts) == total_source_rows
- Log or warn on any records that don't match known patterns to identify data loss early
- Track 'unmatched' records separately for troubleshooting categorization logic

### Dynamic Range Boundaries
- Calculate clear/write ranges based on actual categorized item counts plus header offset
- Avoid hardcoded row/column limits that may exceed or undershoot actual data volume
- Adjust destination cell clearing dynamically to match processed data length
